queued.title: Options strategy expansion — execution path + directional plays
queued.what: AMD put (+$275, 39.6%) outperformed all 13 equity positions combined. Options are currently hedge-only (protective put screener). No execution path exists. Build: (1) options order submitter for Alpaca paper, (2) covered call screener for income in CALM regimes, (3) directional options plays (call/put spreads) as strategy agent alternative to equity entries. Regime detection (HMM + VIX + pre-market macro) is more useful for options than equities — directly informs whether options are cheap or expensive.
queued.prereq: Verify Alpaca paper supports options orders. Protective put screener already producing candidates.

queued.title: CVaR Risk Metric — tail-risk aware position sizing
queued.what: Conditional Value at Risk: expected loss in worst X% of outcomes. Upgrade from max-loss thresholds to proper tail-risk metric. Compute historical CVaR at 95% and 99% from portfolio returns. Warn when exposure exceeds CVaR budget. v1: daily post-close calculation, feed to Agent-08b. v2: per-position CVaR contribution, regime-conditional CVaR, Monte Carlo simulation.
queued.prereq: Alpaca portfolio history for return series.

queued.title: Hedge-vs-Rebalance Decision Framework
queued.what: When pipeline suggests reducing a position, compare cost-to-sell vs cost-to-hedge-with-puts. High alpha + cheap puts = hedge instead of sell. Low alpha or expensive puts = sell. Implements Paper #5's no-trade zone concept for sell decisions. v1: simple cost comparison in morning_position_reviewer.py. v2: full model with transaction costs, market impact, time decay, asymmetric shock response.
queued.prereq: Q-030 (no-trade zone), protective_put_screener single-symbol mode.

queued.title: GSADF Bubble Detection — explosive price early warning
queued.what: Generalized Supremum ADF test on SPY, QQQ, and held sector ETFs. Detects explosive price behavior (bubbles) before they burst. When bubble detected, proactively tighten circuit breaker thresholds. Weekly diagnostic. v1: GSADF test via arch package. v2: combine with EXCF, track contagion lag, auto-tighten circuit breaker.
queued.prereq: arch Python package or custom GSADF implementation.

queued.title: Deflated Sharpe Ratio for performance evaluation
queued.what: Apply DSR correction (Bailey & Lopez De Prado, 2014) when evaluating strategy performance. Corrects for multiple testing, non-normal returns, data snooping. Probability that observed Sharpe is genuine. Flag when DSR < 0.5. v1: add to performance_aggregator.py. v2: per-strategy DSR for backtester (Q-025), walk-forward DSR, dashboard comparison chart.
queued.prereq: performance_aggregator.py producing Sharpe. Enough trading days for meaningful statistics.

queued.title: Cross-Market Contagion Monitor
queued.what: ARDL-style monitoring of cross-index correlation dynamics. Track whether shocks to S&P 500, Nasdaq, international indices propagate to our sectors with measurable lag. When contagion risk elevated, flag for defensive posture. Extends sector_correlation_monitor.py. v1: add SPY/QQQ/EFA/EEM to correlation matrix with lagged correlations. v2: full ARDL model, regime-conditional contagion.
queued.prereq: Q-029 (sector correlation monitor) as base.

queued.title: LSTM Regime Classifier (R&D)
queued.what: Upgrade from HMM (Markovian, memoryless) to LSTM-based regime detection capturing path dependency. The fRNN insight from Paper #4: decisions should consider the full price PATH, not just current state. Only begin after HMM has 30+ days of data to benchmark against. v1: train LSTM on same data, compare accuracy. v2: replace HMM if meaningfully better.
queued.blocker: Need 30+ days of HMM regime data to benchmark against.
queued.prereq: Q-028 (HMM) running 30+ days. PyTorch or TensorFlow for LSTM training.

queued.title: Bull/Bear adversarial debate in Thesis agent
queued.what: Add a two-pass adversarial debate pattern to Agent-06 (Thesis). After forming the initial thesis, run a second pass that argues against it — forces explicit bear case argumentation before finalizing conviction. If the bear case is stronger, downgrade conviction. Inspired by TauricResearch/TradingAgents bull/bear researcher pattern. Implementation: two-pass prompt within existing Agent-06 (not a new agent). Pass 1 forms thesis as today. Pass 2 receives the thesis and constructs the strongest bear case. Final output includes both arguments and adjusted conviction. Reduces confirmation bias in stock selection.
queued.blocker: Need 1+ week of paper trading data to compare thesis quality before/after.
queued.prereq: Research v2 stable. Pipeline producing daily thesis_packet.json with real data. Validate with pipeline-validate.sh before deploy.

queued.title: Reflection memory — narrative-indexed decision history with P&L feedback
queued.what: Store per-symbol per-day reflection records that capture the full decision context: thesis narrative, alt-sentiment snapshot (crowd mood, urgency signals), key technicals at decision time (RSI, relative strength), the decision made + conviction level, and outcome (P&L at +1/3/5 days). Index on narrative regime, not just numbers — BM25 text search on narrative fields naturally surfaces emotional regime matches (FOMO squeeze, fear-driven oversell, earnings euphoria). At thesis time, Thesis agent retrieves 3 most similar past situations and their outcomes. One-line lesson-learned field generated after outcome is known. Records built from artifacts already produced (research_packet, alt_sentiment_packet, thesis_packet, execution_log, trade_journal). No new data sources needed — just persistence of what's currently consumed and discarded.
queued.blocker: Need 1-2 weeks of paper trading data to populate reflection records. No value without historical decisions to retrieve.
queued.prereq: Pipeline executing trades daily. trade_journal.py producing P&L data. Research v2 + Alt-Sentiment producing rich narrative data. BM25 library (rank_bm25 pip package).

queued.title: Autonomous strategy backtester — autoresearch pattern for trading
queued.what: Autonomous strategy optimization loop inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch. Agent proposes strategy parameter changes, runs backtest on historical data (walk-forward, no future sight), measures Sharpe/profit factor, keeps improvements, discards regressions, loops forever. 3-file architecture: prepare.py (download historical OHLCV via yfinance, split train/test windows), strategy.py (the file the agent modifies — entry/exit rules, position sizing, risk parameters), program.md (agent instructions defining the optimization loop + results tracking). Walk-forward validation prevents overfitting to one market regime. No GPU needed — backtesting is CPU-only.
queued.blocker: Need stable pipeline + enough paper trading data to validate backtested strategies against real performance.
queued.prereq: Stable pipeline (orchestrator proven). yfinance installed (done). Historical OHLCV corpus downloaded.

queued.title: Systems tab — strategy toggles, performance reports, parameter history
queued.what: New first-class tab in the Vercel trading app. Strategy on/off toggles (equity execution, options wheel, options BPS) with confirmation UX. Weekly BPS performance report viewer (rendered from bps_performance_report_YYYY-MM-DD.md). Parameter change history timeline. Sector/symbol win rate heatmap. Tunables tab may merge into this. Premium design consistent with art-direction pass. Build after BPS has 1-2 weeks of real data.
queued.blocker: BPS module needs 1-2 weeks of data first
queued.prereq: BPS module running in production (crons active, weekly reports generating)

queued.title: Trading system isolation — dedicated agent + on/off switch
queued.what:
Move all 18 trading cron jobs from the main agent to a new dedicated trading agent with its own isolated workspace. Goal: pause or resume the entire trading system as a unit instead of toggling 18 jobs individually.

Current state: all trading cron jobs run under main. OpenClaw has no native agent start/stop (openclaw agents supports add/delete/bind only). Cron disable is ID-by-ID only — no bulk or agent-scoped toggle.

Proposed design:
1. Create trading agent in openclaw.json with dedicated workspace (~/.openclaw/agents/trading/workspace)
2. Re-point all 18 TradingBot cron jobs to agentId: trading
3. Migrate relevant state files to new workspace
4. Build trading-pause.sh / trading-resume.sh shell scripts that bulk-disable/enable all trading job IDs via openclaw cron disable <id> — since no native group toggle exists, script the IDs
5. Optionally: wire pause/resume to a single gate file (trading_active.json) checked by the pre-market agent — more elegant but step 4 works immediately

Jobs to migrate (18 total): Preflight 6am goals, Morning brief curator, trading:premarket-phase, Morning brief composer, trading:preopen-gateway, TradingBot Market Status, Universe, Research, Alt Sentiment, Thesis, Strategy, Validation, Broker Auth, Risk Decision, Systems Audit, Remediation, Risk Approval, Pre-Open Refresh, Execution, Positions (x3), Volatility (x2), EOD Decision, Post-Close, Daily Audit, Governance Review.

Backup: unchanged — prompt files stay in OpenClaw-s-Brain on GitHub.

Prereq check needed: confirm openclaw cron edit supports changing agentId on existing jobs, or whether jobs must be deleted and re-added.
queued.prereq: Confirm openclaw cron edit supports agentId changes on existing jobs.

queued.title: YouTube video production pipeline — ElevenLabs → video → captions → thumbnail → upload
queued.what:
Automated production pipeline that takes a finished script (from Q-016) and outputs a published YouTube video. Human input is already done at this stage — this is pure production automation.

STAGE 1 — VOICE: ElevenLabs Professional Voice Clone. Jacob records 30+ min of clean audio (reading any natural text — doesn't need to be scripted). Submit via ElevenLabs API to create a persistent voice model. All future voiceover generation uses this model — one-time setup cost, infinite reuse. API key already configured in OpenClaw. Output: MP3 voiceover file per script segment.

STAGE 2 — VISUALS: Three-layer composition.
(a) Screen recordings: auto-capture from terminal sessions using asciinema or OBS scripted capture. Triggered during actual pipeline runs — real terminal output, real state files, real code. Not staged.
(b) Architecture diagrams: generate Mermaid diagrams from the pipeline's agent topology, animate transitions with ffmpeg. These are accurate because they're generated from the actual prompt files and cron jobs.
(c) B-roll transitions: AI video generation (Runway ML, Pika, or similar) for abstract visual segments between technical sections. Keep to <20% of total runtime — the real footage is the differentiator.

STAGE 3 — ASSEMBLY: ffmpeg pipeline stitches audio + screen recordings + b-roll per script timing cues. Script markdown format includes explicit segment markers: [SCREEN:filename], [BROLL:prompt], [DIAGRAM:agent-topology]. Agent reads markers and assembles accordingly.

STAGE 4 — CAPTIONS: OpenAI Whisper transcribes final audio and generates .srt file. Burn captions into video with ffmpeg. Style: clean sans-serif, high contrast, lower-third placement. Not Comic Sans. Not rainbow colors. Minimal and professional.

STAGE 5 — THUMBNAIL: AI image generation (DALL-E or Stable Diffusion via API) using a consistent brand template. Template elements: dark background, bold title text overlay, small brand mark. Batch generate 3 variants per video, pick best. Consistent visual identity across all thumbnails is more important than any individual thumbnail being perfect.

STAGE 6 — UPLOAD: YouTube Data API v3. Upload video, set title/description/tags from script metadata. Description template: hook paragraph, timestamps (auto-generated from script segment markers), GitHub repo link, Ko-fi/donation link, affiliate links (Alpaca, ElevenLabs, Anthropic API). Schedule publish time rather than immediate upload — Tuesday/Thursday 9am ET performs well for technical audiences.

Phased build: Stage 1 (voice clone) + Stage 6 (upload) first. Then Stage 4 (captions). Then Stage 5 (thumbnail). Stages 2+3 (visuals) last — start with screen recordings only before adding AI b-roll.

Monetization hooks baked into every video: GitHub repo link (builds stars/credibility), Ko-fi link ($5 prompt pack access), affiliate links. These go in the description template from day one.
queued.prereq: Q-016 content trigger agent producing well-structured scripts. ElevenLabs Professional Voice Clone recorded (30+ min audio). YouTube channel created. YouTube Data API v3 credentials. Ko-fi or Gumroad account for donation/paid access link.

queued.title: Paper-mode governance tolerance — stop letting bookkeeping artifacts block paper runs
queued.what:
The governance layer is calibrated for live money. In paper mode, reporting artifacts and remediation bookkeeping issues should track and warn, not hard-block approval. Today's example: a missing broker EOD snapshot from 2026-03-06 created a remediation carry-forward loop that blocked three paper sessions. No trade was at risk. No real money was at risk. The system blocked anyway.

Root issue: every governance failure is treated as equally hard regardless of whether it's a safety issue (execution risk) or a bookkeeping issue (reporting gap). That distinction matters in paper mode.

Implementation: add a paper_mode_governance block to risk_policy.json with two sub-lists:

paper_hard_blocks (always kill approval gate, same as live):
- HALT circuit breaker state
- live_trading_enabled=true with any execution attempt
- daily loss limit breach
- VALIDATION_FAIL_CONTAMINATION (bad picks still matter for learning)
- broker_auth_ok=false

paper_soft_blocks (degrade to WARN, track in remediation, do not kill approval):
- UNRESOLVED_CRITICAL_REMEDIATION where issue origin is a reporting/logging artifact (not an execution failure)
- SESSION_CLOSE_RESTRICTED_GATE beyond 1 session carry-forward
- MSG_POLICY_VIOLATION from upstream audit misclassification
- REPEATED_PARTIAL_UPSTREAM when upstream partials are data-richness adds (research_packet, alt_sentiment) not core signal artifacts
- INHERITED_REDUCE_ONLY from prior-session reporting gap (retain REDUCE_ONLY sizing, but do not block approval)

Agent-08b (Risk Approval Gate) needs to read paper_mode_governance from risk_policy.json. When live_trading_enabled=false, check each OPEN remediation item's issue_code against the two lists. Hard blocks kill approval as today. Soft blocks append a PAPER_MODE_GOVERNANCE_WARN issue but allow AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER to proceed.

Also update Agent-10 (Remediation Manager) to tag each tracked item with a governance_class field: SAFETY or BOOKKEEPING. This makes the distinction explicit in the artifact rather than forcing Agent-08b to infer it from issue codes.

Do not remove the tracking or the remediation discipline. Issues still get logged, escalated, and resolved. The only change is that BOOKKEEPING-class issues in paper mode do not hard-block the approval gate.
queued.prereq: Agent-08b prompt (v1.0). Agent-10 prompt (v1.2). risk_policy.json schema update needed.

queued.title: Intraday execute-if-approved mode (Option C)
queued.what:
Extend the late-session paper validation chain to include an execution step after approval. Today's late-session run proved the validation → approval path works (AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER, 2026-03-09). The missing piece is the execution leg.

Specific gap: Agent-12 reads preopen_refresh.json for execution_allowed. That file is not written in the current late-session chain, so Agent-12 fails on MISSING_ARTIFACT before it can place any orders.

Fix: add Step 7.5 to late_session_paper_validation_v1.md — write a minimal late-session preopen_refresh.json with execution_allowed=true only when approval_state=AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER and circuit_breaker_state != HALT. Then run Agent-12 against it. Agent-12 reads risk_decision.json (approved names + sizes), broker_auth_ok.json, and risk_policy.json — all of which are already written by the existing chain.

End-to-end flow when complete:
1. Manual trigger (Telegram command or openclaw cron --at)
2. Fresh scan → thesis → strategy (basket correlation) → validation (WEAK_EDGE gate) → risk eval → approval gate
3. If AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER: write late-session preopen_refresh.json → Agent-12 → paper orders placed
4. Write execution_log.json with picked names, order IDs, fill status
5. Summary posted to channel: names, approval state, order outcome

Constraints to bake in:
- max 1-2 names (hard cap, already in chain)
- minimum session time remaining: skip execution if < 30 min to close (no point entering)
- QUARTER sizing mandatory (REDUCE_ONLY inherited)
- paper only (live_trading_enabled=false gate)
- no order submission if market_status shows circuit_breaker_active=true

Test plan: run on a day where market is open, trigger manually at ~11am or 2pm, verify execution_log.json and broker paper order both appear. Do not test near close.
queued.prereq: late_session_paper_validation_v1.md exists (done 2026-03-09). Approval path proven (done 2026-03-09). Agent-12 prompt already handles paper execution path.

queued.title: Governance doc cleanup — sync paperwork to current runtime state
queued.what:
OpenClaw flagged after the 2026-03-09 fix push that several status docs, manifests, and architecture notes in OpenClaw-s-Brain are behind the current runtime state. This is not blocking any run, but the divergence will cause confusion when investigating future incidents — old notes will look like live state. Scope of cleanup:

(1) Agent-08 / Agent-08b split docs — any architecture overview, pipeline topology diagram, or stage-sequence reference that still describes a single monolithic Agent-08 needs to reflect the split: Agent-08 (Risk Evaluation, 08:08, produces risk_evaluation.json) and Agent-08b (Risk Approval Gate, 08:20, produces risk_decision.json). These are distinct agents with distinct outputs and different models (gpt-5.4 vs gpt-5-mini).

(2) Prompt version labels — Agent-06, Agent-07, Agent-09, and Agent-10 are internally versioned at v1.3, v1.3, v1.2, and v1.2 respectively as of 2026-03-09, but the filenames still carry v1.1. Any manifest, index, or reference doc that tracks prompt versions by number needs to be updated. Alternatively, consider renaming the files to match their internal version (separate decision — see note below).

(3) MSG_POLICY_VIOLATION fix notes — any verification doc or known-issues list that records MSG_POLICY_VIOLATION as an open or unresolved problem should be updated to reflect the root cause (Agent-09 was auditing its own outbound policy against upstream artifact alerts) and the fix (Agent-09 v1.2 split outbound audit from upstream alert allowlist).

(4) UNRESOLVED_CRITICAL_REMEDIATION carry-forward fix notes — same as above. The 2026-03-06 session_close criticals (STOP_ORDER_FAILURE, URGENT_ENFORCEMENT_MISS) were manually adjudicated 2026-03-09. The root cause (Agent-10 not reading session_close.json acknowledged_issues) is fixed in Agent-10 v1.2. Any known-issues or open-items list referencing this loop should be closed.

(5) Cron job manifest / prompt source map — the prompt source drift fix on 2026-03-09 updated three jobs in jobs.json from stale workspace .txt prompts and gpt-5.3-codex to canonical .md repo prompts and gpt-5.4. Any documentation of what each scheduled job runs (prompt path, model) should reflect the corrected state.

(6) Monday go/no-go runbook — ~/.openclaw/workspace/trading-bot/runbooks/monday-go-nogo-checklist.md has several notes marked 'first run post-fix' and 'never produced under new split' for risk_evaluation.json and risk_decision.json. After the first successful Tuesday run (or whichever trading day follows 2026-03-09), update: remove the 'NOTE: This file does NOT exist yet' caveat on Step 9, update KNOWN ISSUES table to reflect resolved items, and note the confirmed proof point.

(7) Pipeline health / governance overview — if there is a top-level architecture doc describing the full 16-agent pipeline, check it against the current agent list, stage sequence, output artifacts, and model assignments. Several agents have changed model and prompt version since initial setup.

Approach: do a targeted pass through OpenClaw-s-Brain/System/ and /workspace/trading-bot/runbooks/ looking for stale references. Do not rewrite working docs — only update the specific fields and notes that are factually behind. Mark resolved items as resolved, update version numbers, update model references. Keep the actual runtime-critical prompt files untouched during this pass.
queued.prereq: Best done after Tuesday's first successful run proves risk_evaluation.json and risk_decision.json under the new split. That gives accurate 'first seen' dates to put in the docs. Can start the non-runbook sections now.

queued.title: Profit path — trading system product
queued.what: Offer a managed paper→live trading service, signal subscription, or white-labeled pipeline to other traders. Highest upside but significant regulatory surface — must research RIA registration requirements, determine whether signal distribution constitutes investment advice under SEC rules, and draft appropriate disclaimers. Do not build or market this before: (a) 60-day paper gate passed with clean results, (b) LLC formed (Q-007), (c) legal review completed. Strategy: start as a signal transparency product (show the thesis, not a buy recommendation), build track record publicly, consult a securities attorney before any paid offering. Fund with Q-008 or Q-009 first.
queued.blocker: 60-day paper gate not yet passed. Legal review required before any public offering.
queued.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Clean paper trading track record. Securities attorney consultation. RIA registration research.

queued.title: Form LLC
queued.what: Form a single-member LLC to operate all business activities under. Unlocks deductibility of LLM subscriptions (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), hardware (new machine, Section 179), software tools, internet, home office, and education. You do not need revenue to form — you need to be operating in pursuit of profit, which you are. File with Florida Secretary of State, get an EIN from the IRS (free, online, 10 min), open a dedicated business checking account. Total cost: ~$125 filing + $138.75/year annual report.
queued.prereq: Florida: file at sunbiz.org ($125 filing fee, $138.75/year annual report). No state income tax — LLC profits pass through to personal return only. Get EIN at IRS.gov (free, 10 min). Open dedicated business checking account after EIN is issued. Consult a CPA before first tax filing.

queued.title: Tax & accounting setup checklist
queued.what: Set up the financial infrastructure to support the LLC properly and protect deductions. Key steps: (1) Open dedicated business checking — never mix personal and business money, this is non-negotiable. (2) Set up expense tracking — Wave (free) or QuickBooks Simple Start. Save every API invoice, hardware receipt, and subscription confirmation to a dedicated folder. (3) Understand Schedule C — single-member LLC files taxes on Schedule C attached to personal 1040, no separate business return required. Florida has no state income tax so no state business return either. (4) Document pursuit of profit — keep a simple log of hours worked, goals, and business activities. Protects against IRS hobby classification. (5) Section 179 for hardware — new machine can be fully expensed in year one rather than depreciated. (6) Find a CPA before first tax filing — not before forming the LLC, but before filing. Early year losses (pre-revenue) can be carried forward to offset future income, but only if IRS agrees it's a business. CPA helps document intent. Deductible categories: LLM API subscriptions, hardware, software tools, internet (business %), home office (dedicated space %), education, domain/hosting.
queued.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Dedicated business bank account opened.

queued.title: AI automation consulting / agency
queued.what: Sell the skillset, not a product. You know how to build multi-agent systems with real infrastructure — most businesses don't have anyone who can do this. One retainer client at $3-5k/month covers all subscriptions and hardware. Services: custom agent pipelines, OpenClaw-style automation setups, AI workflow integration for small businesses. No product needed to start — just a landing page, a LinkedIn presence, and one warm outreach campaign. Strategy: close 1-2 clients to fund the hobby side, keep scope tight, productize repeatable work into templates over time.
queued.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Simple contract template. Define service scope and pricing before first outreach.

Added kill_running_session() to orchestrator for timeout cleanup. Zombie reaper tightened 30m→15m. Routing policy upgraded to v3. Gemini 2.5 Flash live and verified.

Status vocab enforcement across all prompts and Python scripts — no more EXECUTION_FAILED for gated non-execution, consistent SKIPPED_GATED and ERROR_INTERNAL. Integration test suite covering clean happy path, stale artifact contamination, invalid policy, upstream risk halt. See refactor plan Phase 5A-D.

Update Python scripts: order_submitter.py, broker_reconciler.py, preflight_auth.py, performance_aggregator.py, positions_fetcher.py, volatility_fetcher.py. Update all agent prompt output paths. Write dual (run-scoped primary + legacy root-state compatibility mirror) until migration complete. Add bin/run_paths.py helper with get_run_dir() and get_artifact_path(). See refactor plan Phase 4A-C.

completed.title: Trading pipeline Phase 3 — 4-gate Python scripts + health simplification
completed.what: Create bin/gate_session.py (validates session_context.json), bin/gate_artifacts.py (validates artifact_manifest.json freshness for active run), bin/gate_policy.py (validates normalized_risk_policy.json). Four canonical gates replace scattered check logic across agents. Systems auditor simplification: remove PRECEDENCE_BREACH as standalone critical gate, health verdict only HALTs for unique unsafe conditions not already covered by gates 1-4. Remediation simplification: carry-forward warnings force REDUCE_ONLY not HALT; PRECEDENCE_BREACH from prior runs doesn't cascade as fresh CRITICAL each run. See refactor plan Phase 3A-F.

completed.title: Trading pipeline Phase 2C — preflight bootstrap validation

Gates the morning run on bootstrap (07:30 cron) having completed successfully. Non-blocking for the morning run but hardens the safety contract. See refactor plan Phase 2C.

completed.title: Persistent story agent — continuously generates YouTube scripts from the real journey
completed.what:
A background agent that runs on a schedule (nightly or on git push to OpenClaw-s-Brain) and turns the real, ongoing story of building this system into a backlog of staged YouTube scripts. Jacob does not trigger this manually — it runs whether he thinks about content or not. By the time the recording setup is ready, there should be 30-50 scripts waiting.

SOURCE MATERIAL (what the agent reads):
- Git commits to OpenClaw-s-Brain (commit messages + diffs — this is the primary story log)
- queue.json completed[] section (milestone log — what shipped and when)
- trading-bot/state/ files (approvals, rejections, circuit breaker changes, new incidents)
- runbooks/ (decisions documented, go/no-go assessments)
- The running story-arc.md doc (see below) — what has been told, what is next
Do NOT read raw Claude Code session JSONL files — they contain API keys and sensitive data.

STORY ARC DOCUMENT (story-arc.md in the private repo):
The agent maintains a living document that tracks: where we are in the journey (days since first paper trade, paper gate progress, key milestones), what has already been scripted (prevents repetition), what narrative threads are open (e.g., 'governance loop introduced ep 3, fix shown ep 5, proof shown ep 7'), and what the next 3 most interesting things to cover are. Every script generation updates this document. This is what makes the scripts feel like a series instead of isolated videos.

SCRIPT OUTPUT FORMAT (one .md file per script):
```
---
episode: <N>
series: building-ai-trading-system
topic_tags: [governance, incident, agent-10]
status: DRAFT
generated_at: <ISO date>
story_arc_position: <where this fits in the narrative>
title_variants:
  - <option 1 — curiosity gap>
  - <option 2 — direct/technical>
  - <option 3 — result-first>
thumbnail_brief: <text overlay + emotional direction + visual concept>
description_seo: <first 150 chars must contain primary keyword, full description below>
tags: [comma, separated, youtube, tags]
chapters:
  - 0:00 Hook
  - 0:30 Context
  ...
community_question: <specific question to end the video with>
---

[HOOK]
... (first 30 seconds — most interesting thing first, never 'in this video')

[CONTEXT]
... (setup, what system is, why this session matters — assume smart viewer, no prior context)

[CORE]
... (real content with segment markers)
[SCREEN:filename] for terminal/code captures
[DIAGRAM:agent-topology] for architecture visuals
[CARD:topic] where a future video should be linked
[BROLL:description] for transition visuals
[PATTERN_INTERRUPT] every 2-3 min — flag where pace/visual should shift

[TAKEAWAY]
... (honest assessment, what comes next in the arc)

[OUTRO]
... (value-first CTA, cross-link to related episode, community question)
```

YOUTUBE STRATEGY RULES baked into the agent prompt (not vague instructions — explicit rules):
1. Hook rule: open with the most interesting single thing — the failure, the number, the discovery. Never open with context or setup.
2. Title rule: curiosity gap + specificity. 'The fake error message my AI generated about itself' > 'Bug fix video'. Numbers always help.
3. CTA rule: first subscribe ask comes AFTER the first real value delivery, not at the start. Viewers who get value first are 3x more likely to subscribe.
4. Chapter rule: every script gets timestamps auto-generated from section markers. YouTube actively pushes chaptered videos.
5. Cross-link rule: plant [CARD:topic] markers where related content should be referenced. Use natural language: 'I go much deeper on X in episode N — link in the description.' Card library builds as scripts accumulate.
6. Pattern interrupt rule: flag [PATTERN_INTERRUPT] every 2-3 minutes. Production team changes visual or cuts to screen recording to reset attention.
7. SEO rule: description first 150 chars contain primary keyword — that is what appears in search results. Write this line first.
8. Community rule: every video ends with one specific, answerable question. Not 'let me know what you think.' Something like: 'What would you have done differently when the remediation loop first fired on day 1?'
9. Series arc rule: every script references at least one prior episode and teases at least one future episode. Builds watch time across the channel.
10. Honesty rule: this is the differentiator. The script must reference real file names, real error codes, real decisions. Generic descriptions get cut. Vague language gets cut.

REPO STRUCTURE (private GitHub repo: youtube-content):
```
scripts/
  chronological/          # 2026-03-09-governance-loop-incident.md
  by-topic/
    trading-pipeline/
    incidents-post-mortems/
    architecture/
    build-log/
  series/
    building-ai-trading-system/  # episode sequence, numbered
assets/
  thumbnail-briefs/       # one per script
  diagram-specs/          # mermaid specs auto-generated
meta/
  story-arc.md            # living document, updated each run
  keyword-research.md     # YouTube search term targets, updated monthly
  content-calendar.md     # what is ready to produce vs. staged
```

RUN SCHEDULE: Nightly cron via OpenClaw (low priority, runs after midnight ET). Also triggered on git push to OpenClaw-s-Brain when commit message contains significant keywords (fix, incident, proof, complete, approved). Generates 1-3 scripts per run depending on how much happened. Never generates more than 3 per run — quality over volume.

BRAND VOICE embedded in agent prompt: Jacob Barkley — developer building a real AI trading system in public. Not a trading tips channel. Not a generic AI tutorial. A transparent engineering journal with a financial edge. Tone: direct, technically honest, occasionally self-deprecating about failures, never polished or corporate. The audience is developers and technically curious people, not retail traders looking for stock tips.

FIRST SCRIPTS TO GENERATE (day 1 backlog already exists from 2026-03-09):
1. Architecture explainer — the 16-agent pipeline, how it works, why it is built this way
2. Governance loop incident post-mortem — the fake error that blocked 3 days of paper trades
3. Basket quality fix — why XOM+CVX was a redundant bet and how we fixed the selector
4. The paper vs. live governance calibration problem — why the system was too safe for its own good

completed.title: YouTube content trigger agent — 5 bullets in, publish-ready script out
completed.what:
The content layer of the YouTube pipeline. Automation handles production (Q-017) but content must be anchored in real human experience — this agent bridges the two. Jacob writes 5 bullet points in 5 minutes describing what happened, why it matters, and what was learned. The agent produces a full publish-ready video script.

Why this is not fully automated: AI-generated scripts from source code alone produce dry, generic, commodity content. The 5-bullet input from Jacob is the non-negotiable human layer that makes the content real. It preserves authenticity — the thing that actually separates this channel from the hundreds of AI trading bot channels that will never build an audience.

INPUT FORMAT (Jacob provides):
- What happened (1 sentence)
- Why it matters to the system (1 sentence)
- What was surprising or hard (1 sentence)
- What the fix or decision was (1 sentence)
- What a viewer should take away (1 sentence)
Optional: list of relevant source files or state files to include as context.

AGENT READS (automatically, no manual input):
- Relevant agent prompt files from OpenClaw-s-Brain (inferred from bullet content)
- Relevant state JSON files from trading-bot/state/ (for real data to reference)
- queue.json completed section (for accurate history/timeline)
- Pipeline architecture (agent list, stage sequence, models)

OUTPUT: Structured script markdown with:
- HOOK (0:00-0:30): opening that leads with the most interesting thing — the failure, the discovery, the result. Never start with 'in this video I will show you.'
- CONTEXT (0:30-2:00): brief setup, what the system is, why this session matters. Assume viewer knows Python/AI basics but not this specific system.
- CORE (2:00-8:00): the actual content. Walk through what happened with real references to code, state files, and architecture. Segment markers for screen recordings and diagrams: [SCREEN:filename], [DIAGRAM:topology], [BROLL:description].
- TAKEAWAY (8:00-9:30): what this means for the broader project, what comes next, honest assessment of current state.
- OUTRO + CTA (9:30-10:00): subscribe, GitHub link, Ko-fi link, next video tease.
- METADATA block: suggested title (3 variants), description (full, with timestamps auto-filled from segment markers), tags, thumbnail text suggestions.

SERIES TEMPLATES (agent picks the right one based on bullet content):
- POST-MORTEM: incident → root cause → fix → lesson. Best for governance failures, bugs, unexpected blocks. Highest retention because failure is interesting.
- BUILD LOG: here is what I added, why I added it, how it works. Best for new features (Agent-06 basket correlation, etc.).
- DAY-N RECAP: what the system picked today, how it performed, what the approval gate did. Recurring format, builds longitudinal audience investment.
- EXPLAINER: deep dive on one concept (multi-agent systems, risk approval gates, paper trading architecture). Evergreen content, high search volume.

QUALITY RULES baked into the agent prompt:
- Never open with 'in this video' — lead with the most interesting thing
- Write for someone who is smart but has never seen this codebase
- Reference real numbers, real file names, real decisions — not generic descriptions
- Honest about failures and limitations — this is the differentiator vs. polished demo channels
- Script should sound like Jacob talking, not like a Wikipedia article
- Target runtime: 8-12 minutes for explainers/post-mortems, 4-6 minutes for day-N recaps

Brand positioning this content builds toward: Jacob Barkley — developer building a real AI trading system in public, showing what actually works and what breaks. Not a trading tips channel. Not a generic AI tutorial channel. A transparent engineering journal with a financial edge.

completed.title: YouTube / content + tools
completed.what: Document the build publicly. 'I built a 16-agent AI trading pipeline' is genuinely interesting content with a real audience. Monetizes through AdSense, sponsorships, and naturally leads to selling templates, prompt packs, or the OpenClaw setup as a product. Lowest regulatory risk, lowest startup cost, builds audience before you have anything to sell — audience is the asset. Strategy: start with build-log style videos (what you built, why, what broke), layer in tutorials as the system matures, eventually gate advanced content or tools behind a paid tier.

Agent-08b was already correct. Confirmed via openclaw cron list — all three now show gpt-5.4.

completed.title: Agent-09 v1.2 + Agent-10 v1.2 — governance loop fixes
completed.what: Fixed two bugs that caused today's paper session to block on false positives. (1) Agent-09 was applying its own outbound messaging restrictions as a global audit policy against all upstream artifact alerts — risk_status_ping from Agent-06's strategy_spec alerts was being flagged as MSG_POLICY_VIOLATION (CRITICAL). Fixed by splitting Step 6 into an upstream alert allowlist (risk_status_ping explicitly authorized) and Agent-09's own outbound policy. (2) Agent-10 was not reading session_close.json, so criticals acknowledged at session close were carried forward as OPEN indefinitely. Fixed by adding session_close.json as optional input and resolving carry-forward items that match acknowledged_issues[]. Both prompt changes pushed to OpenClaw-s-Brain. State manually adjudicated: open_critical_count 2 → 0, circuit breaker HALT → REDUCE_ONLY.

completed.title: Agent-06 v1.3 + Agent-07 v1.3 — basket quality improvements
completed.what: Agent-06 v1.3: added primary_thesis_driver field per strategy item and STEP 3b basket correlation review. Items sharing the same primary_thesis_driver are de-duplicated — lower-conviction item excluded unless genuinely differentiated by sector, catalyst type, or hold horizon. Would have dropped CVX today (same OIL_REGIME_STRENGTH driver as XOM, no differentiated edge). Agent-07 v1.3: added 9th validation dimension edge_quality (STRONG | ACCEPTABLE | WEAK). WEAK_EDGE rule: thesis_alignment=PARTIAL AND warn_details >= 2 distinct concerns => rating auto-upgraded to FAIL. Would have failed GAP today (PARTIAL alignment + 2 distinct warns). Both changes pushed to OpenClaw-s-Brain commit 5bb4147.

completed.what: Built complete options module as independent sidecar (trading-bot/options/). Follows Signal Bus + Module pattern — reads equity pipeline signals read-only, writes to own namespace. Components: options_screener.py (yfinance chains, IV proxy + snapshot history, earnings blackout, OI/spread/delta/bid filters, equity signal cross-reference), gate_options_risk.py (deterministic capital/concentration gate — max 20% per CSP, max 2 concurrent, 15% cash buffer), options_order_submitter.py (OCC symbol formatting, Alpaca paper Level 2, 09:35 post-open delay), Agent-17 prompt (qualitative screening), Agent-18 prompt (CSP/CC strategy decisions, position management rules). 3 cron jobs: 08:22 screener, 08:28 strategy, 09:35 execution.
